items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

gallery.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.
The exterior of a weathered regional general store with two dilapidated petrol pumps out the front.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.
The exterior of a weathered regional general store with two dilapidated petrol pumps out the front.

items.title: Teenagers brandish knives, hammer at Canberra shopping centre

children.content: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on outside a shopping centre.

items.title: Driver sentenced to 13 years' jail over hit-and-run of Canberra schoolboys

children.content: It's almost a year to the day since Tayler Hazell drove a stolen car erratically through Canberra, seriously injuring two children.

image.altText: Three police officers stand talking seriously next to a road with cones on it.

items.title: 'Forget it': NSW beachgoers urged to cancel plans amid hazardous weather

children.content: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

image.altText: A large wave with surfers and swimmers beyond it and houses seen in the distance.

items.title: 'Electrons to the people:' Neighbours build commercial solar farm

children.content: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

image.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

image.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

items.title: For Australia, the end of the Iran war might just be the start

children.content: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home

children.content: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

image.altText: Tiger Woods of the Jupiter Links Golf Club plays a shot from a bunker

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

children.content: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

image.altText: A Taliban soldier operates an anti-aircraft gun on a clear day.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce to help autistic kids thrive?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

image.altText: An over the shoulder photo of a young girl's hands playing with putty

items.title: As music festivals collapse, one scrappy underdog is making a lot of noise

children.content: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a chaotic indoor music event with a band on stage and a man crowdsurfing amid a lively crowd

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a chaotic indoor music event with a band on stage and a man crowdsurfing amid a lively crowd

avatar.alt: Jeremy Story Carter, with black hair and beard and a red flannel shirt

items.title: 'Idiocy and dullness disease': How a 28yo pushed his community to rethink dementia

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

image.altText: Two tubes of sunscreen with 50+ SPF showing.

items.title: The nine books that knocked our critics' socks off this month

children.content: Wondering what to read next? Gothic crime fiction, a cult Japanese bestseller and a fantastical twist on the Blitz are among our critics' latest recommendations.

gallery.altText: A series of nine book covers on a peach-coloured background.

thumbnail.altText: A series of nine book covers on a peach-coloured background.

items.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from March

children.content: The year might've kicked off with a flood of great records, but March is when things really snap into gear.

image.altText: Collage of musicians Robyn, Courtney Barnett, Bad//Dreems and Snail Mail.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 80s punk rock, Harry Potter spoilers and that internet dress

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

gallery.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

thumbnail.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

items.title: 'It's about fairness': Australian Iranians hit back at 'racist' visa ban

children.content: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

image.altText: A family outdoors smiling

items.title: Iran's soccer team honours victims of deadly airstrike on primary school

children.content: Iran soccer players hold small backpacks to honour the victims of a deadly missile strike on a girls' primary school, before a friendly against Nigeria.

image.altText: Iran's players sing national anthem holding school bags

items.title: 'Stay in your lane': experts urge against following running influencer advice

children.content: Recently, online discussion has turned to what some perceive to be a spate of influencers getting injured.

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

image.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show after mother's disappearance

children.content: Savannah Guthrie is scheduled to return to her job as a co-anchor of NBC's Today show on April 6, more than two months after her 84-year-old mother's unresolved abduction.

image.altText: Savannah Guthrie (left) with her mum (right).

items.title: Blueberry industry under scrutiny amid rapid growth

children.content: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

gallery.altText: Blueberries on a bush in greenhouse.

thumbnail.altText: Blueberries on a bush in greenhouse.

items.title: Man interviewed on suspicion of sex trafficking in Mohamed Al-Fayed investigation

children.content: Former Harrods boss Mohamed Al-Fayed, who died in 2023, has been accused of rape, sexual assault, sexual exploitation and human trafficking on a large scale.

image.altText: A close up image of the face of Mohamed Al Fayed wearing a grey blazer jacket

items.title: Cotton and Davis involved in post-match spat after 36ers level NBL finals

children.content: Sydney coach Brian Goorjian insists he's not concerned by Kendric Davis's post-match tirade at Adelaide superstar Bryce Cotton, following the 36ers' NBL championship-levelling 91-89 win over the Kings.

image.altText: Tempers flare in the last quarter between Bryce Cotton of the Adelaide 36ers and Kendric Davis of the Sydney Kings

items.title: Signs point to measles cases spreading undetected in NSW

children.content: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

gallery.altText: Gloved hands prepare a needle for vaccination.

thumbnail.altText: Gloved hands prepare a needle for vaccination.

items.title: Waratahs snap losing streak with upset win over Brumbies in Canberra

children.content: NSW Waratahs coach Dan McKellar heralds his side's much-needed 30-28 victory against bitter rivals the ACT Brumbies as a special memory for the club.

image.altText: Andrew Kellaway of the Waratahs celebrates with teammates after scoring a try

items.title: Broncos claim pressure-relieving victory over Dolphins, ending tough week on a high

children.content: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

image.altText: Kotoni Staggs dives with the ball

items.title: Socceroos snatch late win as teen debutant impresses heading into World Cup

children.content: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

gallery.altText: Socceroos player Jordy Bos has a finger pointed in the air and smiling as he celebrates a goal

thumbnail.altText: Socceroos player Jordy Bos has a finger pointed in the air and smiling as he celebrates a goal

items.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants

children.content: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

image.altText: Darcy Moore heads into the locker rooms with an injury

items.title: 'There will be tax reform' but treasurer says no decision yet on capital gains

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers commits to tax reform in his upcoming budget, but adds that no decision has yet been taken as to whether capital gains tax will be part of those changes.

image.altText: A man with short, dark hair, wearing a suit jacket and tie, looking serious.

items.title: Barnacle-covered rare turtle washed ashore makes full recovery

children.content: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

image.altText: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery
